zh.Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛
zh.Ashington, Northumberland, England: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德阿生頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset於1429年出生在英國薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛時，他的父親Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington32歲，母親Jane de Harcourt18歲。他於1460年與Jane Sydenham在英國薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1458年被列為sheriff of somerset & dorset。他去世於1463年享年34歲。

zh-hans.Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛
zh-hans.Ashington, Northumberland, England: 英国英格兰諾圖波藍德阿生頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset于1429年出生在英国薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛时，他的父亲Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington32岁，母亲Jane de Harcourt18岁。他于1460年与Jane Sydenham在英国薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1458年被列为sheriff of somerset & dorset。他去世于1463年享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barbe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Laurent, Andre, Gillis, Jean Pierre, Serge, Sylvie, Thierry, Yves.
French: descriptive nickname for someone with a beard, Old French barbe (from Latin barba).
French (Barbé): adjectival derivative of 1, meaning ‘bearded’.

variants.name: Barb
meanings: German: variant of   Barbe  . This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace).
Romanian: variant of   Barbu  .

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Barbon
meanings: English: habitational name from Barbon in Westmorland. The placename is probably from Old Norse bjórr ‘beaver’ + brunnr ‘stream’, alternatively from Old English bera ‘bear’ + burna ‘stream’.
English: nickname from Middle English bare bon ‘bare bone’, perhaps denoting a thin or bare-legged man.
French and English (of French Huguenot origin): nickname for a bearded man, from an augmentative of French   Barbe  .

variants.name: Barby
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Otto, Wolfgang.  French Celestine, Pierre.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Saxony-Anhalt.
French (mainly Dordogne): derivative of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Angelle, Marcel.
French: diminutive of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbot
meanings: French:
from the Old French female personal name Barbot, a diminutive of Barbe , a short form of Barbara .
nickname for someone who tended to speak indistinctly, from French barboter ‘to babble’, or for someone with a beard, from a diminutive of Old French barbe ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbay
meanings: French: variant of Barbé (see Barbe ).

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English reve, refe ‘reeve’ (Old English gerēfa). In medieval England a reeve was an official responsible for the administration of a manor, including organizing work done by the peasants on the land for their lord, collecting rents, selling produce, and so on.
Irish: variant of   Reeves   5.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

variants.name: Sherriff
meanings: English: variant of   Sheriff  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Somerset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

habitational name from the English county of Somerset, which takes its name from Somerton in Somerset (see Summerton ) + Old English sǣte ‘settlers, dwellers’. The Somerset family who are Dukes of Beaufort are descended from the Plantagenets. The surname was assumed by Charles Somerset (1460–1520), illegitimate son of Henry Beaufort, Duke of Somerset. The latter was himself descended illegitimately from Edward III through John of Gaunt. A later member of the family was Fitzroy Somerset (1788–1855), who, as Lord Raglan, was the commander of British forces in the Crimea, one of the men responsible for the charge of the Light Brigade.

possibly a habitational name from one or more places called with Old Norse sumar ‘summer’ + sætr ‘shieling, hill pasture’, such as Summerseat in Elton (Lancashire), but decisive northern early bearers are lacking.

variants.name: Summerton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Somerton, particularly in Oxfordshire and Somerset, but also in Lincolnshire and Norfolk, named with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Summersett
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Somerset  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ali, Hossein, Abbas, Behnaz, Gitti, Hamid, Jafar, Mansour, Masoud, Mehdi, Pari, Rana.  Jewish Jechiel, Bracha, Herschel, Morry.
Iranian: from Zand, the name of a dinasty which ruled Persia 1751–94. It is based on Middle Persian zand ‘great, powerful’ and was originally the name of a former Iranian tribe.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Sand  .

variants.name: Sand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bertel, Ejnar, Lars, Levinus, Nels, Niels, Thor.

English, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a patch of sandy soil, from the vocabulary word sand (German Sand). As a Swedish name it was often purely ornamental, and as a Jewish artificial. It is also found in France (see 3 below).
English: habitational name from a place such as Send (Surrey) or Sound (Cheshire), named with Middle English sand, sond ‘sand’.

variants.name: Zant
meanings: German: variant of   Zand  .
Dutch: variant of   Van Zandt  .

variants.name: Zandi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mahmud, Taher, Ahmad, Azi, Bahram, Behzad, Farman, Firooz, Hamid, Hossein, Jaleh, Masood.
Iranian: derivative of   Zand  , denoting descent from or association with a Persian dinasty of that name.
Italian (Emilia): perhaps a nickname from the dialect word gianda ‘acorn’.

nameMeanings.name: Dorset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  See  Dorsett  .

variants.name: Dorsett
meanings: English: habitational name from the county of Dorset, named from Old English Dorn, an early name of Dorchester (of British origin, from durn ‘fist’, probably referring to fist-sized pebbles) + sǣte ‘dwellers’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: name bestowed in honour of the medieval poet Dante Alighieri ( 1265–1321 ), also used in the English-speaking world. As a medieval given name Dante was a contracted form of Durante ‘steadfast, enduring’. In an English context it was famously borne by the pre-Raphaelite poet and painter Dante Gabriel Rossetti ( 1828–1882 ), the son of an exiled Italian scholar and patriot.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset was born in 1429, in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England, his father, Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington, was 32 and his mother, Jane de Harcourt, was 18. He married Jane Sydenham in 1460, in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. In 1458, at the age of 29, his occupation is listed as sheriff of somerset & dorset. He died in 1463, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset est né en 1429, à Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Jane de Harcourt, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Jane Sydenham en 1460, à Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. En 1458, à l’âge de 29 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of somerset & dorset. Il est décédé en 1463, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset 1429 in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington 32 und seine Mutter Jane de Harcourt 18. Er hat 1460 in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England Jane Sydenham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. 1458, als er 29 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of somerset & dorset aufgeführt. Er starb 1463 im Alter von 34 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset è nato il nel 1429 nel Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England, il suo padre, Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Jane de Harcourt, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Jane Sydenham nel 1460, nel Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Nel 1458, all'età di 29 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of somerset & dorset. Morì nel 1463, all'età di 34 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorsetが1429年にイギリスソメルセトチルトン カンテロで生まれた時，父Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashingtonは32歳，母Jane de Harcourtは18歳であった。1460年にイギリスソメルセトチルトン カンテロでJane Sydenhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。1458年，29歳の時，職業はsheriff of somerset & dorsetと記録されている。彼は1463年に34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset(이)가 1429년 영국 서머세트 치턴 캉로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane de Harcourt(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1460년 영국 서머세트 치턴 캉로에서 Jane Sydenham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1458년, 29세의 나이로 그의 직업은 sheriff of somerset & dorset이었다. 그는 1463년 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset nasceu em 1429, em Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Jane de Harcourt, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Sydenham em 1460, em Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Em 1458, com 29 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of somerset & dorset. Ele faleceu em 1463, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset родился в 1429 г. в местности чилтон кантелоу, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Jane de Harcourt, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Sydenham в 1460 г. в местности чилтон кантелоу, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1458 году, в возрасте 29 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of somerset & dorset. Он умер в 1463 г. в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset nació en 1429, en Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Saint Barbe Esq of Ashington, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Jane de Harcourt, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Jane Sydenham en 1460, en Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. En 1458, a la edad de 29 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of somerset & dorset. Murió en 1463, a la edad de 34 años.
